name: memory-curation
description: Guidance for deciding what should become durable research memory.
instruction: Preserve durable findings, reusable comparisons, limitations, and stable preferences. Compress or exclude low-signal tool chatter and duplicated evidence.
guidelines: Prefer storing synthesized findings over raw dumps when both convey the same information.
Keep negative results, caveats, and unresolved questions if they will help future sessions.
Discard ephemeral coordination text and repeated snippets that do not improve future retrieval.
